policy: Generated from current macro configuration. Historical analogs compare configuration patterns, not isolated chart shapes.

analogs.label: Inflation shock / real-yield repricing
analogs.pattern: Restrictive money and inflation pressure can compress multiples even while leadership remains resilient.
analogs.difference: Current credit stress is more contained and AI infrastructure leadership remains concentrated.
analogs.portfolioLesson: Do not assume multiple expansion while real yields remain restrictive; size risk around inflation and rate sensitivity.

analogs.label: Funding stress without immediate broad collapse
analogs.pattern: Strong risk appetite can coexist with funding pressure until transmission becomes visible.
analogs.difference: Current physical and AI infrastructure cycle differs from late-1990s internet leadership.
analogs.portfolioLesson: Watch funding and volatility carefully; contained credit can delay but not eliminate repricing.

analogs.label: Oil shock / physical constraint recession
analogs.pattern: Physical constraints and inflation pressure interacted with restrictive policy and equity drawdown risk.
analogs.difference: Current physical pressure includes power, copper, and AI infrastructure rather than oil alone.
analogs.portfolioLesson: Physical constraints can convert inflation into margin pressure; infrastructure exposure matters.

analogs.label: Real-rate reset
analogs.pattern: Restrictive real rates forced repricing across duration, credit, and cyclical exposure.
analogs.difference: Current leadership is more concentrated around technology infrastructure.
analogs.portfolioLesson: Treat duration and weak balance sheets cautiously while real-rate pressure remains high.

missingEvidence: Pre-1960 proxy normalization for credit spreads
Comparable real-yield proxy before TIPS era
Power / electricity demand long-history comparable series
